notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.newActivity: New activity will show up here.

applicants.title: Search applicants by name or ID
applicants.body: Type any part of a full name or university ID to instantly filter the list. Results update live as you type — no need to press Enter or submit anything.
applicants.tip: The search is case-insensitive. Clearing the field restores all applicants immediately.

applicants.title: Flag filter — focus on your shortlist
applicants.body: Click the flag icon to show only the applicants you have personally flagged for follow-up. Click it again to return to the full list. Flags are private to your account and persist across sessions.
applicants.tip: You can flag any applicant by expanding their row and clicking the flag icon inside.

applicants.title: Advanced filters — narrow down fast
applicants.body: Open the filter panel to filter by major, nationality, CGPA range, attendance status, CV presence, shortlist/rejection status, languages, skills, expected graduation, and more. Multiple filters stack — only applicants matching ALL selected criteria appear.
applicants.tip: Active filters show as blue chips at the top. Click × on any chip to remove it, or "Clear all" to reset everything.

applicants.title: Sort by any column
applicants.body: Click any column header — Name, University ID, Nationality, CGPA, Major, or Status — to sort the list by that column. Click the same header again to reverse the sort order. An arrow indicator shows which column is active.
applicants.tip: CGPA sorts highest-first by default. Sorting is applied on top of any active filters.

applicants.title: Expand a row — full applicant profile
applicants.body: Click the ↗ expand icon on the right of any row to open that applicant's full profile. Inside you can: view their QR code, download their CV, see all personal and academic details, and take actions — shortlist, reject, flag, or undo any of those.
applicants.tip: Shortlisted and rejected statuses are visible to all company managers. Flags are private to you only.

applicants.title: Register & confirm attendance
applicants.body: "Register an Applicant" opens a QR scanner — scan a student's printed QR code to link them to your company's applicant list. "Confirm Attendance" scans their QR a second time to mark them as physically present at the fair. Both actions require camera access.
applicants.tip: Allow camera permissions when prompted. You can also confirm attendance from inside the expanded applicant row.

managers.title: Search companies by name
managers.body: Type any part of a company name to instantly filter the list. Results update live as you type. The search matches against the full registered company name.
managers.tip: Search is case-insensitive and works alongside any active filters.

managers.title: Filter companies
managers.body: Open the filter panel to filter by attendance status (Pending / Confirmed / Canceled), sector (Private / Federal / Semi / Local), city, industry fields, whether they have registered applicants, and reminder email status. Combine multiple filters to zero in on a subset.
managers.tip: Use "Reminder Status: Not Sent" to quickly surface companies that haven't been contacted yet.

managers.title: Send confirmation reminder emails
managers.body: Opens a modal listing all pending companies. Select individual companies or use "Select All Pending" to batch-select, then click Send. Each selected company receives a confirmation link by email. Sent timestamps appear next to each company.
managers.tip: Only companies with Pending status appear in the reminder modal. Already-confirmed companies are excluded.

managers.title: Sort companies by any column
managers.body: Click any column header — Company Name, Email, Representatives, Sector, City, No. of Applicants, or Status — to sort. Click again to reverse. Use "No. of Applicants" to see which companies are attracting the most student interest.
managers.tip: Sorting by Status groups Confirmed, Pending, and Canceled companies together for easy batch review.

managers.title: Expand a company row — full profile & actions
managers.body: Click the ↗ expand icon on any row to open the full company profile. View their delegate list, preferred majors, opportunity types, and applicants. You can also directly change their attendance status — Confirm, Cancel, or Reset to Pending — from this panel.
managers.tip: Status changes take effect immediately. The company receives no automatic notification when you change their status manually here.

/.title: Applicants
/.tagline: Browse, search, and action everyone who registered.
body: This is the live list of everyone who registered for the Job Fair. Search by name (matches are highlighted), filter, and open any applicant to see their full profile, CV, and QR ticket.
As a company you can shortlist, flag, or reject applicants — each action is confirmed in your notification bell. CASTO sees every applicant across all companies.

/managers.title: Companies & Cooperations
/managers.tagline: Every registered company, searchable and expandable.
body: Every registered company in one place. Search by name (matches highlight as you type), filter by sector or field, and expand a company to see its profile, representatives, applicants, and admin actions.
Use Send Reminders to nudge companies, and open a company card for full details or to cancel/delete a record.

/statistics.title: Statistics
/statistics.tagline: Aggregate insight across applicants and companies.
body: Aggregate insight across all applicants and companies — demographics, majors, GPA spread, attendance, and more. Use the filters to slice the data.

/company-status.title: My Status
/company-status.tagline: Your booth, passes, schedule, and check-in.
body: Your company's home for the event. The Overview tab shows your profile and applicant count; the Event Day tab shows your booth QR, banners, parking slot, entry passes, the event schedule, and lets you raise special requirements.
When you arrive, check in by scanning your booth QR or tapping "I've arrived" on the Event Day tab.

/company-settings.title: Company Settings
/company-settings.tagline: Profile, login access, and display preferences.
body: Edit your company profile, manage login access, confirm your attendance status, and adjust display preferences (font and size).

/event-settings.title: Event Settings (Operations)
/event-settings.tagline: The operational hub for event day.
body: The operational hub for event day: booth assignments and floor map, banners, special requirements, equipment & logistics, delegate lists, attendance & check-in, the schedule, and access passes.
Every change is stamped with who made it and shows up in the Activity Log. Companies see the relevant pieces live on their own Status page.

/event-admin.title: Event Admin
/event-admin.tagline: Report, team & roles, imports, and previews.
body: Higher-level event administration: the post-event report, team & roles (who owns which module), importing companies, the activity log, and the View As preview tool.
Reassigning a module owner is gated behind a two-step verification and notifies the team of exactly what changed.

/view-as.title: View As (Preview)
/view-as.tagline: See what companies, staff, and students see.
body: Preview exactly what a company, an attendance staffer, or the public student check-in sees — read-only, without touching your real session or any data.

/surveyResults.title: Survey Results
body: Responses to the post-event company survey, aggregated for review.

/dev.title: Developer — Email Activity
/dev.tagline: Confirm nothing is emailing real people.
body: Shows whether outbound email is on or off (controlled by EMAIL_ENABLED in the backend), the From address, and a log of recent send attempts — sent vs skipped.
Use this to verify that testing (registering students, sending reminders) isn't emailing anyone while email is off.

profile.title: Company profile
profile.subtitle: Your public details, shown to CASTO and used to match applicants.

attendance.title: Attendance status
attendance.subtitle: Let CASTO know whether your company is attending this year's fair.
attendance.hint: Changing this notifies CASTO. You can update it any time before the event.

access.title: Manage login access
access.subtitle: Anyone with an approved email can log in with your company's shared password.
access.emptyHint: No additional emails yet. Add colleagues who need access below.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Font and text-size preferences, applied across your dashboard.
appearance.scopeNote: This preference is saved to your account and applied every time you sign in.

venue.floorMapSubtitle: Click a booth to manage its assignment
venue.floorMapNote: Schematic layout — will be replaced by the real venue map. Hover a booth for details, click to manage it.

survey.pageTitle: Exit Survey of Internship and Career Fair 2025
survey.processing: Hold on while we are processing your response

hero.title: Job Fair
hero.descriptionLogin: Use the credentials shared with you via email to access the participants of the event
hero.descriptionSignup: Register your company to access full features and connect with potential candidates.

login.title: Log in to your account
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to access your dashboard

validation.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address
validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 6 characters

intro.responseSubmitted: Your response has been submitted. You will be redirected in a few seconds…

requests.doneMessage: Request submitted — CASTO will review and follow up. Thank you!

toasts.sent: Request sent — CASTO will review it
toasts.failed: Couldn't send your request. Please try again.

equipment.notePlaceholder: Anything CASTO should know (e.g. deliver before 8am)

requirement.describePlaceholder: e.g. bringing a catering truck and need floor access, or a dedicated 30A power line.

parking.needsPlaceholder: e.g. 2 cars for delegates, or a larger bay for a branded truck.
parking.hint: Let CASTO know how many vehicles and any special access needs.

empty.booth: No booth assigned yet. Once the CASTO office assigns your booth, its number, zone, map location, and check-in QR code will appear here.
empty.banners: No banners or branding submitted yet. The CASTO branding officer will add your roll-ups, backdrops, and artwork here as they're processed.
empty.requirements: No requirements yet. Use the Requests tab to raise one — it appears here with its status once the CASTO logistics officer picks it up.
empty.equipment: No equipment allocated yet. Tables, chairs, power, and screens the CASTO office assigns to your booth will be listed here.

booth.scanHint: Scan at your booth to confirm attendance.

companyStatus.checkedInToast: You're checked in — welcome to the Job Fair!
companyStatus.notSetUpYet: The CASTO office hasn't set up your booth, branding, or passes yet. Each section below shows what's coming — it'll fill in automatically once CASTO assigns it. You can already view the schedule, and use the Requests tab to ask for equipment, raise a requirement, or send a parking note.
